plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to zero.
Trim QQQI to 10% and SPY to 20% to reduce risk.
Buy CRM and CEG, strong scanner setups, at 10% each.
Hold CRWD at 10% for quality tech exposure.
This plan builds a cash buffer and reduces overall risk.
Focuses on capital preservation with limited upside.
conservative.read: Targets ~1.5% monthly return with low drawdowns. Prioritizes capital preservation by cutting leverage and reducing concentration. Still carries market risk.

plan: Trim SOXL to 2% and sell SPAL completely.
Reduce SPY to 25% and QQQI to 10% to lower risk.
Add to CRWD to 15% as it's working well.
Buy CRM and TECS at 10% each from strong scanner setups.
This balances index exposure with quality tech and some leverage.
Aims for steady growth while managing risk.
balanced.read: Targets ~2.5% monthly return with moderate risk. Balances growth with risk management by trimming leverage and concentration. Still subject to market volatility.

plan: Increase SOXL to 8% and add SPAL to 4% for leverage.
Trim SPY to 15% and sell QQQI to zero to focus on tech.
Add to CRWD to 15% and buy CRM and NVDU at 10% each.
Concentrates capital in high-conviction tech and leveraged ETFs.
This plan aims for aggressive growth through leverage and concentration.
Expect higher potential gains but also significantly higher drawdowns.
aggressive.read: Targets ~4% monthly return, accepting significantly higher risk and drawdowns. Leverages strong tech setups and ETFs, but daily rebalancing can cause decay. High potential for loss.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and high-beta tech (CRWD).
Sell QQQ to free up capital for income generation.
Increase QQQI to 30% and trim SPY to 20%.
Buy COP at 20% as a dividend-paying energy stock.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly income over growth.
Focuses on capital preservation with reliable distributions.
income.read: Targets ~1% monthly return, prioritizing stable income and low drawdowns. Shifts focus to income-generating assets like QQQI and dividend stocks. NAV can still fall in market downturns.
